---
title: String formatting in R and Python
date: "2024-11-24"
categories: [code, r, python]
draft: true
---

In this post, we will go through **string formatting** in R and Python. Spoiler alert: In R, we're going to use `sprintf()` function to format strings, as there is no native formatting support as in Python. Python offers more options, from C-style formatting (using the `%` operator), to `str.format()` method, and everyone's favorite `fstrings`.


String formatting refers to styling text (and numerical data) to appear in a desired format. This includes adding padding to align content to the left, center, or right of a given space or character, adding units, other other symbols by transforming the content.
